parsed_review.summary: CP00-306 is a clean descriptor-only CRM UI binding-tail closeout pack covering the planned 10 RP09 units (RP09.P04.M05.S16-RP09.P04.M06.S05, risk A). Identity, range, unit count, deliverable distribution {fixture:1,test:1,hermes_evidence:1,claude_review:1,implementation:2,ui:4}, and micro-title distribution {Permission And Audit Binding:5, Synthetic Fixture Set:5} are mutually consistent across the prompt baseline, manifest (plan_binding_snapshot + included_units), review-baseline, registry CRM_CORE_CP306_PACK_BINDING, crm-core-contract current_pack/ui_binding_tail_descriptor, closeout-pack-plan.json, and next-pack-queue.json (live_latest=CP00-305, preview=CP00-306); deviation_from_plan is false everywhere. The implementation is descriptor-only: service.js freezeCp306Result sets exhaustive false flags (no crm/campaign/proposal/referral/ui/command runtime, no state/audit/object-storage writes, no Hermes runtime receipt), every row is descriptor_only:true with runtime_execution:false, and validators.js enforces per-row descriptor-only/customer-safe/no-raw-payload guards unconditionally (fail-closed) plus backed no-leak guards (empty_state count leak, denied_state permission-decision exposure, fixture payload, Hermes packet body, unauthorized data). Authority boundaries hold: claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, asserted by both the validator and scripts/validate-rp09-crm-core-contract.mjs. Hermes is used only as a deterministic evidence packet (H09.CP00-306, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime, consistent with hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false. CP178+ review hardening is present (forbidden_review_evidence covers auth-failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled tools, missing raw-output/inspection-basis, and trust-claim blockers); this review's own harness passed readiness auth-preflight (exit 0, valid JSON) with read-only tools only and forbidden_tools_present:false. All 7 required public exports are defined (2 in service.js, 5 in validators.js) and re-exported via index.js wildcard (explaining why index.js is unmodified); tests in model.test.js cover coverage validity, descriptor-only rows, evidence packets, and handoff (to CP00-307 / RP09.P04.M06.S06); command-evidence.json records every expected validation command at exit_code 0 with Hermes gate H09 passed. The manifest correctly claims production_ready:false / implemented_pending_review — the production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification marker, not an enterprise-trust claim, and Hermes production_ready_candidate:true is appropriately gated on coverage+tail validity. The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present, which is expected and explicitly noted as post-review gated in command-evidence.json. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approver. One non-blocking P3 advisory on fail-open conditional guard structure is noted; no P0/P1/P2 issues found. The pack supports closeout pending the human/control-plane finalization steps downstream of this review.

findings.title: Defense-in-depth row guards are presence-gated and could fail open if backing fields are removed
findings.details: In validateCrmCoreCp306UiBindingTailDescriptor the per-row semantic guards (synthetic_fixture_binding.real_client_data_loaded, build_smoke.executes_ui_runtime, hermes_ui_evidence.emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, claude_ui_leak_prompt.leak_detected, empty_state.no_unauthorized_count_leak, denied_state.permission_decision_detail_included at lines 1632-1652) use the pattern `if (rows.X && rows.X.prop !== false)`, which only fires when the property exists. For CP00-306 these fields ARE present via the CRM_CORE_CP30x_ROW_EXTRAS chain (service.js:687-691 and 832-835), so the guards are active and the pack's boundaries hold. The structural nit is that the conditional pattern means a future refactor dropping a row-extras field would silently turn the corresponding guard into a vacuous pass (fail open) rather than an error. Blast radius is limited because the unconditional per-row guards at validators.js:1626-1629 (descriptor_only, runtime_execution, customer_safe_errors_only, raw_payload_included) fail closed and already enforce the core descriptor-only/no-runtime boundary for every row.
findings.recommendation: Optionally harden the semantic guards to assert presence-and-value (fail closed), or add a row-extras schema/shape assertion so missing backing fields surface as validation errors. No change required for CP00-306; the pattern matches the accepted CP00-299..CP00-305 packs and the backing fields are present.
findings.post_review_disposition: Advisory only; does not block CP00-306 pack closeout or goal closeout. Core descriptor-only enforcement already fails closed and all backing fields are present, so CP00-306 descriptor-only and no-leak boundaries are intact. Suitable as an optional future hardening item for the shared CRM validator pattern.
